Patch Set 16:

(3 comments)

None of the tests you added seem related to HSM. Is sanity-hsm really the right test suite to target?

New test functions were recently added to manage changelog and changelog users in tests. Maybe sanity (or a new separate test suite sanity-changelog) would be a better fit for those tests?

Patch Set 16:

(3 comments)

Adding a new test script in the auster test suite has a cost. So as agreed with Andreas, we decided to insert these tests in sanity-hsm.sh.

Patch Set 16:

Ping reviewers

Patch Set 16: Code-Review-1

(4 comments)

Sebastien – Thank you for the new tests. I like the additional testing and would like to see this land, but we’ve now introduced some changelog setup, deregister, etc. repeated functionality.

Andreas’s recent patch https://review.whamcloud.com/27535 added several new functions added to test-framework.sh that manipulate changelogs. Now there are functions that can take the place of some of the functions added by this patch; for example the new changelog_clear() can take the place of changelog_clear_mdt(). This could be cleaned up later, but I’d like to see this duplication of functionality be fixed before this patch lands.

I agree that the new changelog tests would be a better fit in some other test script, possibly sanity.sh, but I could live with this patch landing just to get the functionality and changelog coverage.

Patch Set 16:

(4 comments)

OK to use the new functions introduced by patch https://review.whamcloud.com/27535, with some modifications.

commit.subject: LU-9727 tests: exercise new changelog fields and records
commit.message:
LU-9727 tests: exercise new changelog fields and records

Add new tests in sanity-hsm to exercise new changelog fields
and also record types.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-hsm
Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>
Change-Id: I4af3175f3b7b9842412ceb8fe97f85c5c180db26
